title: dryopteris scabra var. caesariana
intro: Dryopteris scabra var. caesariana, also known as the rough buckler fern or rough wood fern, is a variety of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to China and Japan. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Dryopteris scabra var. caesariana often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
